summary: Fair‑quality audience tape of a legendary ’67 set – Golden Road, New Potato Caboose, Alligator and He Was A Friend of Mine all shine despite muddy vocals and bass‑heavy mix.
review: The performance is hailed as a rare glimpse of the early Dead’s high‑energy chemistry: a blistering Alligator, a jam‑filled New Potato Caboose, an exuberant Golden Road and a scorching fragment of He Was A Friend of Mine.  The recording is only marginally clear – bass‑saturated and occasionally muddy – but for a 1967 audience source it remains listenable and beloved by fans.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance (average ~4.6) while noting only modest technical limitations (average ~3.0); weighted average yields a strong 4.2 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bass is over‑saturated, vocals can be muddy and overall clarity is marginal, but the mix lets most instruments be heard and captures the era’s ambience.

standout_songs: He Was A Friend of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: A short four‑song set that flows tightly: He Was A Friend of Mine → Golden Road → New Potato Caboose → Alligator, punctuated by wildly funny LSD‑fuelled banter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is repeatedly called brilliant, especially on the opening solo of He Was A Friend of Mine.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass lines are described as “hot” and “on fire,” notably during Alligator and New Potato.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ is praised for its whizzing, swirling presence throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Kreutzmann drumming is noted as solid; some reviewers debate which drummer is present but agree the percussion drives the performance.
